author: Chris Wolfgang
name: Wolfgang Console App
description: A console application preconfigured with additional package including McMaster's CommandLineUtils, Serilog and others

framework.description: The target framework for the project.

version.description: Initial version number for the project.

author.description: The name of the company or person that authored this project. This name will appear in the <Author> tag in the csproj and in the copyright

description.description: A description of this application. This name will appear in the <Description> tag in the csproj and in the copyright

response-file.description: Response file support. A response file is a text file of command line arguments that users can pass as @file.rsp instead of typing each argument at the command line

choices.description: Each argument in the response file is on its own line
All arguments in the response file are on a single line, separated by spaces

HealthCheck.description: Include a 'health' subcommand that validates runtime dependencies and configuration (with a --json flag for container health probes).

unit-tests.description: Include a companion xUnit unit-test project under tests/ (with InternalsVisibleTo so the app's internal types are testable).

integration-tests.description: Include a companion xUnit integration-test project under tests/ that exercises the app's real composition (config binding + DI).

benchmarks.description: Include a companion BenchmarkDotNet project under benchmarks/ for measuring the performance of the app's hot paths.

otel.description: Include OpenTelemetry tracing + metrics (console exporter by default; OTLP endpoint configurable via AppSettings).

top-level.description: Use top-level statements for the entry point (Program.cs with no explicit Main). The default keeps the classic 'static Task<int> Main' form. Functionality is identical either way.
